text: Dissection began from the inferior part of the mediastinum, and the plane between the mass and the pericardium was carefully dissected with Maryland bipolar forceps.
The dissection was performed between the borders of the bilateral phrenic nose, maintaining safe margins.
Thymic veins were identified, encircled, and ligated using Maryland bipolar forceps or a bipolar energy device.
The right thyrothymic ligament was retracted and divided.
The thymectomy was completed and the specimen was removed from the thoracic cavity using an endobag.
After irrigating the thoracic cavity with iodine solution, the left pleural space was opened and the left phrenic nerve was identified.
The left upper pole of the thymus was dissected and the thyrothymic ligament was retracted and divided.
Fatty tissue in the inferior part of the mediastinum was excised.
A 24 French chest tube was placed through the mediastinum and the operation was concluded.
Here you can see the incision with the chest tube in place.
